description: Add new SVG illustrations to documentation pages that currently have no visuals, following the neo-brutalist design system.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Add progress flow illustration to streaming-progress.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the Streaming & Progress guide, I want a visual diagram of the 6-step progress pipeline so I can quickly understand how progress notifications flow from my tool handler to the AI agent.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `ProgressFlow` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`
It shows the 6-step progress pipeline as a vertical or zigzag flow: Tool handler -> Adapter IIFE (CustomEvent) -> Content script relay -> Extension background (WebSocket) -> MCP server -> AI agent
Each step is a labeled box with a brief description of the transport mechanism (CustomEvent, chrome.runtime.sendMessage, WebSocket, notifications/progress)
The illustration is placed after the 'How Progress Flows' heading (line 116) and before the numbered list (line 120) in `docs/content/docs/guides/streaming-progress.mdx`
The illustration uses CSS variables for theming and follows all neo-brutalist style rules
userStories.notes: The progress flow is described in text at docs/content/docs/guides/streaming-progress.mdx:118-126 as a 6-step numbered list. The illustration should complement (not replace) the numbered list — place it between the section heading ('## How Progress Flows', line 116) and the text 'When your tool calls...' (line 118). Use a vertical flow (top to bottom) since there are 6 steps — horizontal would be too compressed. Each box should be ~160px wide, stacked vertically with arrows between them. Label the arrows with the transport mechanism. Use viewBox approximately 400x500 with max-w-lg. Arrow marker IDs: `pf-arrow`. The top box (Tool handler) should use the primary-highlighted style since that's the developer's code. The bottom box (AI agent) should also be highlighted to show the endpoint.

userStories.title: Add lifecycle hooks sequence illustration to lifecycle-hooks.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the Lifecycle Hooks reference, I want a visual timeline showing when each hook fires in the adapter's lifetime so I can quickly understand the execution order.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `LifecycleSequence` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`
It shows the adapter lifecycle as a vertical timeline: onActivate -> (onNavigate, repeating) -> (onToolInvocationStart -> onToolInvocationEnd, repeating) -> onDeactivate
Registration and Removal phases are visually distinct from the repeating Navigation and Tool phases
The illustration is placed after the 'Hook Lifecycle' heading and introductory text (after line 17) and before the table in `docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx`
All neo-brutalist style rules are followed
userStories.notes: The lifecycle hooks are described in the table at docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx:19-54. The illustration should show a vertical timeline with 5 phases: Registration (onActivate, fires once), Navigation (onNavigate, fires many times — use a repeat indicator), Tool start (onToolInvocationStart), Tool end (onToolInvocationEnd), and Removal (onDeactivate, fires once). Use a vertical layout with boxes connected by a central timeline line. The one-time hooks (onActivate, onDeactivate) should use solid boxes. The repeating hooks (onNavigate, onToolInvocation*) should use a grouped/repeated visual (perhaps a box with a small repeat icon or a dashed border to indicate 'fires multiple times'). Use viewBox approximately 480x380 with max-w-lg. Arrow marker IDs: `lc-arrow`.

userStories.title: Add error categories illustration to error-handling.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the Error Handling guide, I want a visual showing the error categories and their retry behavior so I can quickly choose the right ToolError factory method.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `ErrorCategories` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`
It shows the 6 error categories (auth, rate_limit, not_found, validation, timeout, internal) with their retryable status
Retryable errors (rate_limit, timeout) are visually distinct from non-retryable ones (auth, not_found, validation, internal)
Each category shows the factory method name (e.g., `ToolError.auth()`)
The illustration is placed after the introductory section and before the '## Factory Methods' heading in `docs/content/docs/guides/error-handling.mdx`
userStories.notes: The error categories are described throughout docs/content/docs/guides/error-handling.mdx. The Factory Methods section starts around line 48. The illustration should be placed around line 47, just before '## Factory Methods'. Design: a 2-column or 3-column grid of category cards. Each card is a small neo-brutalist box with the category name in the header, the factory method as code text, and a retryable/not-retryable indicator. Retryable categories (rate_limit, timeout) should have the primary-highlight background. Non-retryable categories should have the default background. Use viewBox approximately 560x280 with max-w-3xl. Consider grouping them visually: left side = 'Not retryable' (auth, not_found, validation, internal), right side = 'Retryable' (rate_limit, timeout) with a visual divider.

userStories.title: Add 'How It Works' illustration to introduction page (index.mdx)
userStories.description: As a new user reading the Introduction page, I want a visual showing the 3-step 'How It Works' flow so I can immediately understand the system at a glance before reading the details.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `HowItWorks` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`
It shows a 3-step vertical or horizontal flow matching the Introduction page's 'How It Works' section: Start MCP server -> Extension connects -> Agent calls tool
Each step has a number, title, and brief description matching the text content
The illustration is placed after the 'How It Works' heading (line 19) and before the numbered list (line 21) in `docs/content/docs/index.mdx`
All neo-brutalist style rules are followed
userStories.notes: The 'How It Works' section is at docs/content/docs/index.mdx:19-25. It has 3 steps: (1) Start the MCP server — discovers plugins, exposes tools; (2) Extension connects — injects adapters into matching tabs; (3) Agent calls a tool — dispatches through extension to tab, result flows back. This is similar in concept to QuickStartFlow but more focused on the runtime flow rather than installation steps. Use a horizontal 3-step layout similar to QuickStartFlow (docs/components/illustrations.tsx:16-189) but with different content. Each box should have: numbered header, title text, and a brief description. The middle box (Extension connects) could be the primary-filled one since it's the core differentiator. Use viewBox approximately 800x160 with w-full. Arrow marker IDs: `hiw-arrow`. Place the illustration between the heading and the numbered list — it provides a visual summary before the detailed text.

userStories.title: Add installation paths illustration to install/index.mdx
userStories.description: As a new visitor reading the Installation page, I want a visual showing the three installation paths (User, Plugin Developer, Platform Contributor) so I can immediately identify which section applies to me.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `InstallPaths` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`
It shows three paths side by side: 'For Users' (simplest), 'For Plugin Developers' (medium), 'For Platform Contributors' (advanced)
Each path shows the key tools needed (e.g., Users: CLI + Chrome; Developers: + Bun + SDK; Contributors: + full monorepo)
The paths are visually progressive (simple to complex, left to right)
The illustration is placed after the introductory text (line 7) and before '## For Users' (line 9) in `docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx`
userStories.notes: The installation page is at docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx. It has 3 sections: 'For Users' (line 9), 'For Plugin Developers' (around line 70), 'For Platform Contributors' (around line 130). The illustration should be a simple 3-column layout showing the three paths as boxes of increasing height or complexity. Left box: 'Users' — npm install, opentabs start. Middle box: 'Plugin Developers' — Bun + SDK + plugin CLI. Right box: 'Contributors' — full monorepo, all build tools. Use viewBox approximately 700x160 with w-full. Each box should use the box-with-header pattern. The 'Users' path could be highlighted with primary fill since it's the most common path. No arrows needed — these are parallel options, not a flow.

userStories.title: Add first plugin workflow illustration to first-plugin.mdx
userStories.description: As a new plugin developer reading the 'Your First Plugin' tutorial, I want a visual showing the 5-step workflow so I can see the full picture before diving into each step.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `FirstPluginFlow` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`
It shows the 5-step plugin creation workflow: Scaffold -> Define Tool -> Register -> Build -> Test
Each step has a number and a brief label
The illustration is placed after the introductory paragraph (line 7) and before '## Prerequisites' (line 9) in `docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx`
All neo-brutalist style rules are followed
userStories.notes: The first-plugin tutorial is at docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx. It has 5 numbered sections: 1. Scaffold the project, 2. Define a tool, 3. Register the tool, 4. Build, 5. Test it. The illustration should be a horizontal flow similar to QuickStartFlow but with 5 steps. Each box is smaller to fit 5 in a row. Use viewBox approximately 900x140 with w-full. The style should match QuickStartFlow: numbered headers, brief command or action in each box. Arrow marker IDs: `fp-arrow`. The 'Build' step could be primary-filled since that's where the magic happens (generates IIFE, registers plugin).

userStories.title: Update docs/CLAUDE.md illustration table with all new components
userStories.description: As a future contributor, I want the docs/CLAUDE.md illustration table to be up-to-date so I know which illustrations exist and can reuse them instead of creating duplicates.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Current Illustrations' table in `docs/CLAUDE.md` lists all illustration components that exist in `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`
Each entry has the component name, the page(s) it's used on, and the concept it illustrates

The table includes all illustrations from PRD 1 (ArchitectureIllustration, ConfigDirectory, MonorepoStructure, DispatchFlow) and this PRD (ProgressFlow, LifecycleSequence, ErrorCategories, HowItWorks, InstallPaths, FirstPluginFlow)
userStories.notes: The docs/CLAUDE.md file has a 'Current Illustrations' table at the bottom that currently lists 3 components (ArchitectureIllustration, QuickStartFlow, PluginStructure). After PRD 1 and the preceding stories in this PRD, there will be up to 13 total illustration components. Update the table to include all of them. Read `docs/components/illustrations.tsx` to get the full list of exported components, then list each one with its usage location and concept. Use repo-root-relative paths for the 'Used on' column (e.g., 'docs/content/docs/guides/streaming-progress.mdx').
